sections.title: Talking to him again before meeting Osman

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: Hello again. Have you spoken to Osman yet? He should be just outside the palace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll go and see him.

sections.title: Talking to him after meeting Osman

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: Hello again. I hear you have agreed to help rescue Prince Ali. On behalf of the Emir, I will have a reward ready for you upon your success.

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: Greetings! I am Hassan, Chancellor to the Emir of Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: Well... we do currently have a very urgent issue we need to resolve, and I suppose you look like someone who knows how to get a job done. Are you definitely interested in helping?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: You'll find our Spymaster, Osman, just outside the palace. Go to him and tell him I sent you. He will fill you in on the details of our problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: I see. Well you know where to find me if you do wish to help us.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You again. How are things going in Draynor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an imprint of the key.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Good. Bring me a bronze bar, and I'll get a copy made.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an imprint of the key.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I'll use this to have a copy of the key made. I'll send it to Leela once it's ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on gathering all the items I need.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Well if you need help, just talk to Leela.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osman? I was told by the Chancellor to come and speak to you. Apparently you have an issue that I can help with.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That would be an apt description. However, I find you to be an interesting choice by the Chancellor. Why has he chosen to trust you with this task over one of our own?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I just asked if he needed help and he said yes.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That man is far too trusting at a time when we must take extra care. However, if he has made his decision, I will not question it. Still, you should know that I will be keeping a close eye on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. So what is this issue?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Prince Ali, heir to the Emir of Al Kharid, has been taken. My spies have already discovered where he is being held, but we need someone to make the rescue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm sure I can manage that. How do I go about rescuing him?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The Prince has been taken by a group of bandits led by the self-proclaimed 'Lady' Keli. They are holding him in the abandoned jail just east of Draynor Village.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: According to our information, Keli is the only one able to freely move around the area. For you to get the Prince out, you will need to disguise him as her. She will of course need dealing with first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why can't I just go in and kill her and her bandits?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: And endanger the life of the Prince in the process? No. There will be no unnecessary risks. We need to do this with as little bloodshed as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You make a fair point. Do you know what Keli looks like?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: She has blonde hair and wears pink clothes. My daughter, Leela, one of my spies, is currently in Draynor Village keeping an eye on the jail. I'm sure she can help you with the specifics.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Before you go, there is one more thing. You'll need a key to get the Prince out of his cell. Keli has the only one. You could steal it, but that seems like a risk we should avoid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if she has the only copy, and I can't just steal it, how do I get the key?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: If you bring me an imprint of the key along with a bronze bar, I can show you how to make a copy. You should be able to make an imprint of the key using some soft clay.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Of course, you'll need to find a way to get Keli to show you the key without causing suspicion. I'm sure Leela can help you with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like I should head on over to Draynor Village and see Leela then.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Indeed. Do you have any further questions before you go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why they've taken the Prince?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: No, but we have our theories.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Care to share them?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: No. You have not yet proven yourself enough to be trusted with that information.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Now, do you have any further questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where abouts in Draynor is Leela?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: She will be somewhere near the abandoned jail that the bandits are using. It's just east of the village.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Do you have any further questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I think I know everything I need to.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Then you should get going.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You're back. Do you have everything needed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll go and prepare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must be Leela. Your father sent me to help rescue Prince Ali.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Yes, he sent word ahead that you'd be coming. Are you aware of the plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to obtain a copy of the key to the Prince's cell, create a disguise for him that makes him look like Keli and then break him out of the jail.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: I'd say that's a good summary. Now, do you have any questions for me about any part of the plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas for the key?

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Keli keeps it on her at all times, on a chain around her neck. If you can convince her to show it to you, you might be able to use some soft clay to take an imprint.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound easy.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: My suggestion is that you pretend to be interested in joining her bandits. From there, you should be able to steer the conversation towards the key.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Once you have the imprint, take it to my father along with a bronze bar. He'll then be able to make us a copy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have the imprint!

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Then you should take it to my father along with a bronze bar. He'll then be able to make us a copy.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Do you have any other questions about the plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas for the disguise?

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: To make the Prince look like Keli, you'll need a blonde wig and a pink skirt. You'll also want some skin paste to hide the black eye he got when they captured him.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: There's an old sailor in the village who makes rope. Perhaps he could make you a wig. Don't forget to dye it once you have one.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: For the skin paste, there's a local witch who's apparently an expert on all sorts of potions. I'm sure she could make you some. I hear she also sells dye, if you need some for the wig.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Finally there's the skirt. I imagine you could just buy one from any clothes shop. Thessalia's Fine Clothes in Varrock is probably the closest.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Do you have any other questions about the plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't think so. I'll go and prepare.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Why, hello there, [lad/lass/kid]. Me friends call me Ned. I was a man of the sea, but it's past me now. Could I be making or selling you some rope?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you make other things apart from rope?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I'm sure I can. What are you thinking of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you knit me a sweater?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Do I look like a member of a sewing circle? Be off wi' you. I have fought monsters that would turn your hair blue.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I don't need to be laughed at just 'cos I'm getting a bit old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about some sort of wig?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well... that's an interesting thought. Yes, I think I could do something. Give me three balls of wool and I might be able to do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I will get some. I think a wig would be useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have them here. Please make me a wig.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Okay, I'll have a go.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Here you go. How's that for a quick effort? Not bad I think!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Ned. There's more to you than meets the eye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't need one right now.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you repair the arrow holes in the back of my shirt?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Ah yes, it's a tough world these days. There's a few brave enough to attack from ten metres away.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: There you go, good as new.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Ned. Maybe next time they will attack me face to face.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't need anything.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make skin paste?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Why, it's one of my most popular potions! Lots of people around here like to pretty their faces up a bit. I can make it for you if you get me what's needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Well dearie, you need a base for the paste. That's a mix of ash, flour and water. Then you need redberries to colour it as you want. Bring me those four items and I will make you some.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Yes I can. I see you already have the ingredients. Would you like me to mix some for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please. Mix me some skin paste.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: That should be simple. Hand the things to Aggie then.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Tourniquet, Fenderbaum, Tottenham, Marshmallow, Marblearch.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: There you go dearie. That will make you look good at the Varrock dances.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you. I don't need any skin paste right now.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Okay dearie, that's always your choice.

sections.title: Talking to her after requesting to join her

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Oh, it's you. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping we could talk more about your prisoner.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Until you join us, I can say little. What I can tell you is that he is very valuable. If all goes well, he will make us very rich.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you the famous Lady Keli? Leader of the toughest gang of bandits around?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Yes, I am Keli. You've heard of me then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heard of you? You're famous in Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: That's very kind of you to say. Reputations are not easily earned. I have managed to succeed where many fail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think Katrine is still tougher.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well you can think that all you like! Those cowards dare not leave Varrock. Out here, I'm toughest. You can tell them that! Now get out of my sight, before I call my guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your latest plan then?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Why do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I was actually hoping to join your group.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Join us? Interesting... I suppose you do look the type.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You'll of course need to properly prove yourself before we let you join us. However, what I can tell you is that we currently have a very valuable prisoner. If all goes well, he will make us very rich.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see. You must have been very skillful.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: To catch him? Oh yes. We had to grab him without his bodyguards noticing. It was a stroke of genius. Mostly my doing, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know someone won't try to free him?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: There is no way to release him. The only key to his cell is on a chain around my neck, and the locksmith who made it died very suddenly.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: There isn't another key like this in the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I see the key please?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It will be something I can tell my grandchildren, when you are even more famous than you are now.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well I suppose there's no harm in letting you see it. After all, you have no hope of stealing it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'd better go now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I touch the key for a moment please?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well... only for a moment then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you so much! You are too kind.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You are welcome, but run along now. I will need some time to consider your request to join us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'd better go now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is a good way to keep secrets.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: It is the best way I know. Dead men tell no tales.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll be off. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must have trained a lot for this work.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: I have used a sword since I was a girl. My first kill was before I was even six years old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't disturb someone as tough as you.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Yes, I am very busy. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard rumours that you kill people.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: There's always someone ready to spread rumours. I hear all sort of ridiculous things these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've never really heard of you.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You must be new around here then. Everyone knows of Lady Keli and her prowess with a sword.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, still doesn't ring a bell.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well, you know of me now. You should also know that I will wring your neck if you don't show some respect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't show respect to killers and hoodlums.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You should, you really should. I am wealthy enough to place a bounty on your head, or I could just remove your head myself. Luckily, I am too busy to deal with the likes of you, so clear off!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I have heard of you. You're famous in Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. Who are you guarding here?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Can't say. It's all very secret. You should get out of here. I am not supposed to talk while I guard.

sections.title: Talking to Leela after having all the required items
Talking to Leela again if the player lost the key

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You're back. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I lost that key you gave me.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Well that was foolish. I can sort you out with another, but it will cost you 15 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got that much.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Then come back to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I have 15 coins.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Now, how are things going with that guard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't spoken to him yet.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Well you'd better get on it then. We need him out of the way.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You're back. Do you have everything needed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do indeed.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good work. Now, before breaking the Prince out, you'll need to find a way to deal with his personal guard. He's talkative, so try to find a weakness. Remember, we don't want any unneeded violence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll go have a chat to this guard.

sections.title: Talking to Joe once he is drunk

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Halt! Who goes there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello friend. I'm just here to rescue the Prince, if thats okay?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Thatsh a funny joke. You are lucky I'm shober. Go in peace, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some beer here. Fancy one?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Ah, that would be lovely. Only one though, just to wet my throat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. It must be tough being here without a drink.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: That was perfect! I can't thank you enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you? Still okay? Not too drunk?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: No, I don't get drunk from only one drink. I reckon I'd need at least two more for that. Still, thanks for the beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you care for another beer, my friend?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: I'd better not. I don't want to be drunk on duty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, just keep these for later. I hate to see a thirsty guard.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Franksh! That wash jusht what I need to shtay on guard. No more beersh, I don't want to get drunk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the life of a guard.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Well, the hours are good, but most of those hours are a drag.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Sometimes I wonder if I should have spent more time learning when I was a young boy. Maybe I wouldn't be here now, scared of Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want to be when you were a boy?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Well, I loved to sit by the lake, with my toes in the water. I'd shoot the fish with my bow and arrow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a strange hobby for a boy.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: It kept us from goblin hunting, which was what most boys did.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Hang on... Why do you ask? What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, chill out. I won't cause you trouble.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Sorry, it's hard to relax when I'm on duty. Stress of the job, and all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why do you do it?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: There's good money in it, and some of the shouting I rather like.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: RESISTANCE IS USELESS!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you buy with your great wages?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Really, after working here, there's only time for a drink or three. All us guards go to the same pub and drink ourselves stupid.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: It's what I enjoy these days. I can't resist the sight of a really cold beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you be interested in making a little more money?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: What? Are you trying to bribe me? I may not be a great guard, but I am loyal. How dare you try to bribe me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, you've got the wrong idea, totally. I just wondered if you wanted some part-time bodyguard work.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Oh... sorry. No, I don't need money. As long as you were not offering me a bribe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Thanks, I appreciate that. Talking on duty can be punished by having your mouth stitched up. These are tough people, make no mistake.

sections.title: Talking to Leela again after speaking to Joe

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You're back. How are things going with that guard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could attack him.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: I don't think that's a good idea. Any violence could put the Prince at risk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep thinking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might be able to get him drunk.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Yes, that could work. I'd imagine three beers would do it. Why don't you give it a try?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could bribe him to leave.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: It would take a lot of gold to convince him to betray Keli. She's not known to be kind to those she believes to be traitors. Perhaps there's something else you could try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You should try talking to him. He might give away some sort of weakness.

sections.title: Talking to Leela once Joe is drunk

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You're back. How are things going with that guard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's been dealt with.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Great! I think that means we're ready. Go in and use some rope to tie Keli up. Once she's dealt with, use the key to free the Prince. Don't forget to give him his disguise so the guards outside don't spot him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! I'm here to tie you up!

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Prince Ali? I'm here to rescue you.

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: Oh thank goodness! What's your plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already dealt with Lady Keli and the guard. I'm going to get you a disguise so the guards outside don't spot you leaving. I'll be back once I have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take this disguise. You can use it to get past the guards outside.

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: Thank you, my friend. I must leave you now, but my father will pay you well for this.

sections.title: Talking to Joe after Prince Ali's escape

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Did yoush say something about shome Prince?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Oh... okay.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You did it! Prince Ali is now safe again. You should head back to Al Kharid. I expect you will be well rewarded for your work.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Prince Ali is safe once more. Chancellor Hassan has your payment.

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: Good to see you, friend. I am forever in your debt for what you did, as is all of Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: I know my father is very grateful as well. If he were in better health, he would have thanked you personally. However, at his age, it is best that he spends his time resting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just happy to have helped. All the best.

lines.speaker: Chancellor Hassan
lines.text: Prince Ali is home safe. You have the eternal gratitude of the Emir for rescuing his son. Please, take this payment as a thank you.
